title: Boat Rental in Vlora: A Budget Guide to Costs and Choices in 2026
html:
Vlora offers some of the most affordable boat rental on the Albanian Riviera, with half-day motorboat hire from EUR 120 and full-day catamaran charters from EUR 450. The main departure points are the Vlora Marina and the quays along the Lungomare promenade. To compare verified listings, you can browse day charters in Vlora on the BoatHire24 marketplace.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Vlora?

Shared boat tours start from EUR 50 per person, while private motorboats cost EUR 120, 350 for a half day. The price depends on boat size, season and whether you hire a skipper. Many operators include fuel for short trips. A typical full-day catamaran charter runs from EUR 450 to EUR 750. Crewed luxury yachts, such as those listed on the Salty Sun Charters charter page, start around EUR 900 per day.

Where to set off in Vlora

Most boat rentals depart from one of four local bases. Vlora Marina is the largest and most modern, with a fuel dock and provisioning shops. Porto Vlora on the south side of the Bay of Vlora offers easy access to open waters. Radhima Bay, a 15-minute drive south, is popular for speedboat rentals and water sports. For a more traditional setting, the quays at Uji i Ftohtë provide direct access to the Karaburun Peninsula. Karaburun Peninsula itself has several secluded anchorages ideal for day trips. The island of Sazan lies 13 nautical miles north-west and is a popular destination for snorkelling tours.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (5, 6 guests), best for exploring the coastline near Radhima; from EUR 120 half-day.
  2. Cabin cruiser (6, 8 guests), ideal for day trips to Sazan Island; from EUR 250 half-day.
  3. Catamaran (8, 12 guests), spacious for families, with swimming platform; from EUR 450 full-day.
  4. Crewed luxury yacht (10, 14 guests), includes captain and host, perfect for special occasions; from EUR 900 full-day.

Costs breakdown

  • Shared group tour: EUR 50 per person for 3-hour trip to the Bay of Vlora
  • Small motorboat (half-day, no skipper): EUR 120, 200
  • Catamaran (full-day, with skipper): EUR 450, 750
  • Crewed Sunseeker or Azimut (full-day, inclusive): EUR 900, 1,500

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Vlora?

No, most rental boats up to 30 hp can be driven without a licence, but for larger boats the operator may require proof of competency. Albanian law allows non-licensed operation of small vessels with limited engine power. Always ask the provider for their specific requirements. Many renters opt for a local skipper to navigate the Bay of Vlora and its coastal winds.

If you want to avoid the afternoon breeze known as the 'Murlan', which picks up around 14:00 in July and August, schedule your trip for a 09:00 departure so you get calm waters in the morning.

Seasonal tips

The high season runs from June through August, when the weather is reliably warm and the sea is calm until midday. The Murlan wind often strengthens from lunchtime, so earlier departures are advisable. Spring (May) and autumn (September) offer lower prices and fewer crowds, though water temperatures are cooler. Many charter companies reduce their rates by 20, 30 per cent outside August. In 2026, the Albanian government expects tourism numbers to rise, so booking a boat in Vlora through day charters in Vlora ahead of time is recommended.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 50 (shared) to EUR 1,500 (luxury yacht)
  • Peak months: June, July, August
  • Trip length: 3 hours (shared) to full day (8 hours)
  • Typical capacity: 5, 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Vlora

Can I rent a boat without a skipper?

Yes, many small motorboats and catamarans can be hired bareboat if you have a valid licence or the provider waives the requirement for low-powered engines.

What is the best time of day to boat in Vlora?

Morning departures between 08:00 and 11:00 offer the calmest sea conditions before the Murlan wind builds up.

Are there booking fees on BoatHire24?

BoatHire24 charges a transparent service fee, and the total price is shown before you confirm the booking.

How far is Sazan Island from Vlora?

Sazan Island is approximately 13 nautical miles from Vlora Marina, a 40-minute journey in a fast motorboat.

Whether you are visiting for a day or a week, renting a boat in Vlora opens up hidden coves and the clear waters of the Karaburun Peninsula.
